title.ko: 전력망; 전력을 공급하거나 분배하기 위한 회로 구성 또는 시스템; 전기에너지를 저장하기 위한 시스템
title.en: ELECTRIC POWER NETWORKS; CIRCUIT ARRANGEMENTS OR SYSTEMS FOR SUPPLYING OR DISTRIBUTING ELECTRIC POWER; SYSTEMS FOR STORING ELECTRIC ENERGY

definition.ko: 회로 구성 배전망을 위한 것; 직류; 교류 H02J1/00; H02J3/00 결합된 것; 특정되지 않은 것 H02J4/00 베터리를 위한 것 H02J7/00 비상 또는 대기 전원 공급을 위한 것 H02J9/00 스테이션의 보조 설비에 전력 공급을 위한 것 H02J11/00 회로상태의 원격지시를 위한 것 H02J13/00 전기 에너지를 저장하는 시스템 H02J15/00 전력의 무선 공급 또는 배전을 위한 회로 구성 또는 시스템 H02J50/00

title.en: Circuit arrangements for DC mains or DC distribution networks
using intermediate DC-AC-DC conversion
Arrangements for reducing harmonics or ripples
Current-controlled supply systems, e.g. constant-current supply systems
Two-wire DC power distribution systems
Three-wire DC power distribution systems; Systems having more than three wires
DC supplies with two or more different DC voltage levels
Parallel operation of DC sources
being switching converters (H02J0001108000, H02J0001120000 take precedence)
having arrangements for blocking reverse current flow, e.g. using diodes (H02J0001120000 takes precedence)
Parallel operation of DC sources having power converters with further DC sources without power converters
Balancing load and power generation in DC networks
characterised by load management
using energy storage units, e.g. batteries or dynamo-electric machines coupled to flywheels

title.ko: 교류간선 또는 교류배전망을 위한 회로 구성
title.en: Circuit arrangements for AC mains or AC distribution networks
Arrangements for handling faults or abnormalities, e.g. emergencies or contingencies
characterised by the contingency detection means in AC networks, e.g. using phasor measurement units [PMU], synchrophasors or contingency analysis
for preventing or reducing power oscillations in networks
Arrangements for selectively connecting one or more loads to one or more power sources or power lines
by providing alternative feeding paths when the main path fails
Circuit arrangements for power supply or distribution technologies responsive to energy trading
Arrangements for reducing harmonics or ripples
using a single network for simultaneous distribution of AC power at different frequencies
Arrangements for connecting networks of the same frequency but supplied from different sources
Controlling the transfer of power between connected networks; Controlling load sharing between connected networks
Synchronisation of networks
Current-controlled supply systems, e.g. constant-current supply systems
Arrangements for adjusting frequency in AC networks, e.g. by control of active power
Arrangements for adjusting voltage in AC networks by changing a characteristic of the network load
by switching loads on to, or off from, the networks, e.g. progressively balanced loading
by adjustment of reactive power
Demand-responsive operation of AC power transmission or distribution networks
responsive to end-user or load operations (H02J0003140000 takes precedence)

title.ko: 전력망 내 무효전력을 조정, 제거 또는 보상하기 위한 구성
title.en: Arrangements for adjusting, eliminating or compensating reactive power in networks
using series compensators, e.g. thyristor-controlled series capacitors [TCSC]
having reactive elements actively controlled by bridge converters, e.g. unified power flow controllers [UPFC] or controlled series voltage compensators
using shunt compensators
with stepwise control, e.g. switched capacitor banks
with stepless control
having reactive elements actively controlled by bridge converters, e.g. active filters or static compensators [STATCOM]
the reactive elements being purely inductive, e.g. superconductive magnetic energy storage [SMES] systems
the bridge converters being multilevel bridge converters or modular multilevel converters
using reactive elements connected in series with semiconductor switches, e.g. static VAR compensators [SVC], thyristor-controlled reactors [TCR] or thyristor-switched capacitors [TSC]
using tap changing or phase shifting transformers
using rotating AC generators, e.g. synchronous generators
the arrangements being an integral part of the loads or of their control circuits
Arrangements for eliminating or reducing asymmetry in polyphase networks
Arrangements for balancing of the load in networks by storage of energy
using dynamo-electric machines coupled to flywheels
using batteries or super capacitors with converting means
Arrangements for transfer of electric power between networks of substantially different frequency
Arrangements for transfer of electric power between AC networks via high-voltage DC [HVDC] links; Arrangements for transfer of electric power between generators and networks via HVDC links
Arrangements for feeding a single network from two or more generators or sources in parallel; Arrangements for feeding already energised networks from additional generators or sources in parallel
Arrangements for the handling of islanding, e.g. for disconnection or for avoiding the disconnection of power
Synchronisation of generators for connection to a network or to another generator
with automatic parallel connection when synchronisation is achieved
with means for ensuring correct phase sequence
Controlling the sharing of generated power between the generators, sources or networks
Scheduling or selectively controlling the operation of the generators or sources, e.g. connecting or disconnecting generators to meet a demand
Controlling the sharing of active power
Controlling the sharing of reactive power

Circuit arrangements for mains or distribution networks not specified as AC or DC; Circuit arrangements for mains or distribution networks combining AC and DC sections or sub-networks (arrangements using intermediate DC-AC-DC conversion H02J0001002000;arrangements using high-voltage DC [HVDC] links H02J0003360000)
using a single network for simultaneous distribution of AC and DC power
Networks integrating separated AC and DC power sections
for transfer of electric power between AC and DC networks, e.g. for supplying the DC section within a load from an AC mains system

title.ko: 배터리를 충전 또는 방전시키거나 배터리로부터 부하에 공급하기 위한 회로 구성
title.en: Circuit arrangements for charging or discharging batteries or for supplying loads from batteries
for charging batteries from AC mains by converters
Regulation of the charging current or voltage
using discharge tubes or semiconductor devices
using discharge tubes only
using semiconductor devices only
using magnetic devices having controllable degree of saturation, i.e. transductors

title.ko: 가변속도로 구동되는 발전기에서 축전지를 충전하기 위한 것, 예. 차량용
title.en: for charging batteries from dynamo-electric generators driven at varying speed, e.g. on vehicle

title.ko: 계자변화에 의한 충전전류 또는 전압의 조정
title.en: Regulation of the charging current or voltage by variation of field
due to variation of ohmic resistance in field circuit, using resistance switching in or out of circuit step by step
due to variation of continuously-variable ohmic resistor
due to variation of make-to-break ratio of intermittently-operating contacts, e.g. using Tirrill regulator

title.ko: 방전관 또는 반도체장치를 사용하는 것(H02J 7/28이 우선)
title.en: using discharge tubes or semiconductor devices

title.ko: 포화도가 제어가능한 자기장치를 이용하는 것 (H02J 7/28이 우선)
title.en: using magnetic devices with controllable degree of saturation
using magnetic devices with controllable degree of saturation in combination with controlled discharge tube or controlled semiconductor device
using armature-reaction-excited machines
for charging batteries from a charging set comprising a non-electric prime mover

title.ko: 저장 장치와 다른 직류원을 모두 사용하는 회로망 내에서의 병렬 운전, 예. 버퍼링을 제공하는 것 (H02J7/14가 우선)
title.en: Parallel operation in networks using both storage and other DC sources, e.g. providing buffering (H02J0007140000 takes precedence)
with light sensitive cells
Arrangements using end-cell switching
characterised by the exchange of charge or discharge related data
with electronic devices having internal batteries, e.g. mobile phones
between battery management systems and power sources
between battery management systems and external servers (batteries used in smart grids for balancing of the load H02J0003320000)
Leader-follower arrangements
Arrangements for checking compatibility or authentication between one component, e.g. a battery or a battery charger, and another component, e.g. a power source
acting upon multiple batteries simultaneously or sequentially
for charge balancing, e.g. equalisation of charge between batteries
Passive balancing, e.g. using resistors or parallel MOSFETs
Active balancing, e.g. using capacitor-based, inductor-based or DC-DC converters
including safety or protection arrangements
against overcharge
against overcurrent
against overdischarge
against overvoltage
against overtemperature
using circuits for correcting or protecting against reverse-polarity
characterised by the mechanical construction
including monitoring or indicating arrangements
Control of state of charge [SOC]
Control of state of health [SOH]
Regulation of charging or discharging current or voltage
with prioritisation of loads or sources
in response to battery current
in response to battery voltage
Circuit arrangements for emergency or stand-by power supply, e.g. for emergency lighting
in which an auxiliary distribution system and its associated lamps are brought into service
in which the distribution system is disconnected from the normal source and connected to a standby source
with automatic change-over
requiring starting of a prime-mover
Circuit arrangements for providing service supply to auxiliaries of stations in which electric power is generated, distributed or converted
Circuit arrangements for providing remote monitoring or remote control of equipment in a power distribution network
characterised by displaying of information or by user interaction, e.g. supervisory control and data acquisition [SCADA] systems
Monitoring network conditions, e.g. electrical magnitudes or operational status
characterised by the transmission of data to equipment in the power network
the power network being locally controlled, e.g. home energy management systems [HEMS]
the power network being controlled at grid-level, e.g. using aggregators
characterised by the remotely-controlled equipment, e.g. converters or transformers
the equipment comprising generators
the equipment comprising loads connected to the power network
the equipment comprising energy storage systems
the equipment forming part of substations
the equipment being switches, relays or circuit breakers
specially adapted for protection systems
the equipment being power outlets

title.ko: 특히 전력망에 적합한 전기 에너지 저장을 위한 시스템
title.en: Systems for storing electric energy specially adapted for power networks
using storage of hydraulic energy
using storage of pneumatic energy, e.g. compressed air energy storage [CAES]
using storage of inertial or mechanical energy, e.g. using flywheels
using coils, e.g. superconductive magnetic energy storage [SMES] systems
using stored hydrogen

title.ko: 전력의 무선 공급 또는 배전를 위한 회로 구성 또는 시스템
title.en: Circuit arrangements or systems for wireless supply or distribution of electric power

definition.ko: 이 메인 그룹에서, 전력 전송에 사용되는 무선 기술의 특정 유형은 그룹 H02J 50/05-H02J 50/30에 포함되고, 그것의 회로 배치 또는 시스템은 그룹 H02J 50/40-H02J 50/90에 포함된다.

title.en: using capacitive coupling
using inductive coupling
of the resonant type
using ultrasonic waves
using microwaves or radio frequency waves
characterised by the type of transmitting antennas, e.g. directional array antennas or Yagi antennas
characterised by the type of receiving antennas, e.g. rectennas
using light, e.g. lasers

title.ko: 둘이상의 전송 또는 수신 장치를 사용하는 것 (H02J 50/50 우선)
title.en: using two or more transmitting or receiving devices (H02J0050500000 takes precedence)
using additional energy repeaters between transmitting devices and receiving devices
responsive to the presence of foreign objects, e.g. detection of living beings
involving the reduction of electric, magnetic or electromagnetic leakage fields
involving the exchange of data, concerning supply or distribution of electric power, between transmitting devices and receiving devices
involving detection or optimisation of position, e.g. alignment
Indexing scheme relating to dispersed electric power generation
Supply or distribution of decentralised, dispersed or local electric power generation
Dispersed power generation using fossil fuels, e.g. diesel generators
Dispersed power generation using renewable energy sources
Solar energy
Photovoltaics
Wind energy
Fuel cells
Renewable hydrocarbon sources
Hybrid power plants, i.e. a plurality of different generation technologies being operated at one power plant
Indexing scheme relating to circuit arrangements for AC distribution networks
Details of circuit arrangements for mains or AC distribution networks
Simulating, planning, modelling, reliability check or computer assisted design [CAD] of electric power networks
Grid-level management of power transmission or distribution systems, e.g. load flow analysis or active network management
Circuit arrangements adaptive to forecasted demand
Circuit arrangements adaptive to forecasted power generation
Indexing scheme relating to spatial reach or load
Networks for supplying or distributing electric power characterised by their spatial reach or by the load
Local stationary networks having a local or delimited stationary reach
supplying households or buildings
being internal to power sources or power generation plants
the load networks being external to vehicles, i.e. exchanging power with vehicles
exchanging power with road vehicles
exchanging power with electric vehicles [EV] or with hybrid electric vehicles [HEV]
characterised by the loads connecting to the networks or being supplied by the networks
Home appliances
Portable electronic devices
Medical devices, medical implants or life supporting devices
for selectively controlling the operation of the loads
for limitation of the power consumption in the networks or in one section of the networks, e.g. load shedding or peak shaving
for partial power limitation, e.g. entering degraded or current limitation modes
according to a non-electrical condition, e.g. temperature
according to an economic condition, e.g. tariff-based load management
Indexing scheme relating to circuit arrangements for communication
Circuit arrangements for communication specially adapted for monitoring, managing or controlling operation of power networks remotely
using wired networks, e.g. data transmission buses or optical fibres
Power line communication [PLC]
using wireless networks, e.g. mobile telephones
involving the use of Internet protocols
